explanations.1: .PHONY tells Make that 'start' is not a real file to create, just a command label to run.
explanations.3: SWT_GTK3=1 and the other assignments set environment variables before running spoon.sh, kind of like passing instructions to the program as it starts.
explanations.5: .PHONY marks 'up' as a command label too, so Make knows you're not trying to create an actual file called 'up'.
